title: Uncertainty-aware gating with Venn-Abers interval width
summary: Calibration was already considered, but Venn-Abers offers more than calibrated means: interval width is an uncertainty signal.
rationale: Calibration was already considered, but Venn-Abers offers more than calibrated means: interval width is an uncertainty signal. That uncertainty can decide when to trust the smooth global classifier versus the sharper causal/ranking experts.
implementation_hint: For each base model or blend candidate, compute fold-wise inductive Venn-Abers calibrated probabilities on OOF predictions and retain both midpoint and interval width. Learn simple regime-specific blending rules such as: trust ranker/pressure models more when classifier interval width is large or when base models disagree strongly; otherwise keep the stable global blend.
expected_effect: Can improve ensemble selection without overfitting blend weights to tiny AUC deltas, and may reduce bad overconfident predictions on out-of-distribution races/regimes.
risk: Extra calibration layers are data-hungry and can be noisy on rare regimes; interval width may correlate with class balance rather than true epistemic uncertainty if bins are too small.
